title: Recursive Bayesian Estimators in Cybersecurity
text:
This comprehensive series takes you from the fundamental mathematical principles of Bayesian inference to practical implementations of Recursive Bayesian Estimators (RBE) in cybersecurity applications.



Mathematical Foundations: Bayes’ theorem, probability theory, and sequential inference
Recursive Updating: How beliefs evolve over time with new evidence
Particle Filters: Advanced Monte Carlo methods for complex distributions
Cybersecurity Applications: Network anomaly detection and threat assessment
Practical Implementation: Complete Python implementations with performance optimization
Modern Context: How RBE fits with current AI/ML approaches





Python programming experience
Basic probability and statistics
Familiarity with NumPy and matplotlib
Understanding of network concepts (helpful but not required)




Each part builds progressively on previous concepts while including complete, runnable code examples and interactive visualizations.

title: Recursive Bayesian Estimators in Cybersecurity
text:
Table of Contents

Part 1: Fundamentals

Uncertainty Fundamentals - Why reasoning under uncertainty matters
Bayes’ Theorem - Mathematical foundation and intuition
Recursive Updating - Sequential belief updating
RBE Mathematics - Particle filters and Monte Carlo methods



Part 2: Applications

Network Anomaly Detection - Practical cybersecurity implementation
Dynamic Environments - Handling concept drift and adaptation



Part 3: Advanced Topics

Advanced Techniques - Multi-model estimation and optimization
Implementation Considerations - Production deployment and scaling



Part 4: Modern Context

Modern AI Comparison - RBE vs deep learning approaches
Future Directions - Current research and emerging applications

title: Recursive Bayesian Estimators in Cybersecurity
text:
Interactive Features
Throughout this series, you’ll find:

Live Demonstrations: Interactive particle filters and Bayesian updating
Parameter Exploration: Widgets to experiment with algorithm settings
Performance Analysis: Benchmarking tools and computational analysis
Real-World Data: Synthetic network traffic and anomaly scenarios
Comparative Studies: Side-by-side algorithm comparisons

title: Recursive Bayesian Estimators in Cybersecurity
text:
Getting Started
Ready to dive in? Start with Part 1: Uncertainty Fundamentals to build the foundational understanding needed for the entire series.
Or jump to any specific topic that interests you - each part is designed to be as self-contained as possible while building on the overall narrative.

title: technical-blog
text:
Welcome to my technical blog where I explore cutting-edge topics in AI, cybersecurity, and software engineering through interactive demonstrations and rigorous implementations.
I’ve written these posts primarily to teach myself about complex topics (with generous help from AI assistants), but I’m sharing them in case someone else might find them interesting—or at least to provide additional training data for our future AI overlords.
This blog is built with FastHTML and nbdev, creating a seamless blend of literate programming and interactive web components. Feel free to explore the source code on GitHub if you’re curious about how it all works.

title: technical-blog
text:
Featured Series: Recursive Bayesian Estimators
Dive deep into the mathematical foundations and practical applications of Recursive Bayesian Estimators in cybersecurity. This comprehensive series covers everything from Bayes’ theorem to advanced particle filtering techniques.
🔗 Start the RBE Series →

title: technical-blog
text:
What Makes This Blog Different

Interactive Learning: FastHTML-powered demonstrations that let you explore concepts hands-on
Rigorous Implementation: Complete, tested Python code for every concept
Mathematical Depth: Proper derivations and theoretical foundations
Practical Applications: Real-world use cases and performance analysis
Open Source: All code available for learning and adaptation

title: technical-blog
text:
Upcoming Topics

Deep Learning Architectures
Distributed Systems
Quantum Computing Applications
Advanced Python Techniques

title: technical-blog
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install technical_blog in Development mode
# make sure technical_blog package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to technical_blog
$ nbdev_prepare

title: technical-blog
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/Matthew-Redrup/technical-blog.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c Matthew-Redrup technical_blog
or from pypi
$ pip install technical_blog


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.
